message:
fix(io): optimize Lines newline scan; broaden process module to non-WASM; harden timer safety

Three targeted fixes:

- io/lines.rs: Replace linear position() scan over buf[bytes_read..]
  with a simple buf.last() == Some(&b'\n') check. The newline can only
  appear at the end of the buffer (enforced by step 4's
  read_until(b'\n')), so scanning from bytes_read was wasteful. This
  reduces per-line overhead from O(n) to O(1).

- lib.rs + process.rs: Widen the process module gate from #[cfg(unix)]
  to #[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))], enabling compilation on
  Windows. Gate unix-specific imports (AsRawFd, RawFd, libc::fcntl)
  behind #[cfg(unix)] and add a #[cfg(windows)] import block for
  AsRawHandle/RawHandle. Provide a no-op set_nonblocking() stub on
  non-unix to maintain compilation.

- time/intrusive_wheel.rs: Add a Drop impl for TimerNode that panics if
  the node is still linked, catching use-after-free bugs at drop time
  rather than silently corrupting the wheel. Promote two debug_assert!
  guards on is_linked() to assert! so they fire in release builds too,
  since inserting an already-linked node is a memory safety hazard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
